steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The students claim broken-wing display is exclusive to shorebirds (order Charadriiformes). To weaken this, the data must show at least one non-shorebird performing the behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'variation among shorebird species': stays within Charadriiformes; doesn't challenge exclusivity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'non-shorebirds that don't perform it': consistent with the claim, not a counterexample. ✗
  • Choice C — 'shorebird frequency data': describes how often shorebirds do it, irrelevant to whether other orders do too. ✗
  • Choice D — 'yellow-billed cuckoo and sandhill crane performing the display': both are non-shorebirds (Cuculiformes and Gruiformes) that exhibit broken-wing display, directly disproving exclusivity. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
